lenses.guidance: v 6's 'restrain the people from bringing' is the narrative mirror of ex32's 'they brought their gold to Aaron.' The structure inverts the calf-frenzy — same impulse, opposite altar. Generosity to Yahweh undoes the gold poured for an idol.

v 6's 'more than enough' gives a worship-model for today. To worship in giving is to risk overflowing the need — to trust that the work needs offerers more than it needs measured-out funds. Faithful generosity is its own response to grace.
